changesProposed.name: Colossal Biosciences

verified.name: Colossal Biosciences
verified.description: American biotechnology company working to de-extinct extinct animals including the woolly mammoth and Tasmanian tiger using genetic engineering and CRISPR technology. The company also develops reproductive technology for conservation biology.
verified.notes: Database entry valuation of $1.5B is outdated. Wikipedia confirms Series C funding of $200M in January 2025 brought valuation to $10.2B, making it Texas' first decacorn. Total raised of $435M confirmed as of January 2025. Company also acquired ViaGen Pets in November 2025 and launched Colossal Australia through TIGRR Lab acquisition in August 2025.

changesProposed.name: Nuro

verified.name: Nuro
verified.description: Autonomous vehicle technology company that initially developed custom autonomous delivery vehicles and has since pivoted to licensing its Level 4 driving system, Nuro Driver, to automakers and mobility providers.

changesProposed.name: OpenAI

current.name: OpenAI
current.founder: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever

verified.name: OpenAI
verified.description: American artificial intelligence research organization that developed the GPT family of large language models, DALL-E series of text-to-image models, and Sora series of text-to-video models. OpenAI is structured as a for-profit public benefit corporation partially controlled by a nonprofit foundation.
verified.notes: Wikipedia indicates OpenAI was founded as a nonprofit in December 2015, with a for-profit subsidiary created in 2019. A 2025 restructuring converted the subsidiary into a PBC 26% owned by the nonprofit. October 2025 share sale valued company at $500 billion. Microsoft investment cited as 'over $13 billion' historically; Wikipedia notes Microsoft owns 27% post-restructuring.
